title: WOS Delivery Config
description: A WOS Delivery Config sidecar document. Consolidates three deployment-environment concerns that share a single tenancy and lifecycle: business calendar (working days, holidays, operating hours for SLA evaluation), notification templates (reusable notice bodies for adverse-decision, hold, appeal, and SLA notifications), and correspondence metadata (templates for cataloging inbound/outbound case communications). Joined to a workflow by `targetWorkflow` URI. Per ADR 0076 D-3: replaces the prior wos-business-calendar, wos-notification-template, and wos-correspondence-metadata sidecars; the schema family lands at six files with the delivery and ontology-alignment sidecars as the only sidecars. At least one of `calendar` / `notifications` / `correspondence` MUST be present; absent blocks indicate a deployment that does not require that concern.

^x-.$comment: Vendor extensions per Kernel spec §10.6 (extensions escape hatch)

$wosDelivery.description: WOS Delivery Config specification version. MUST be '1.0'. Identifies this document as a WOS Delivery Config sidecar (calendar + notifications + correspondence) and pins the specification version. Processors reject documents with unsupported versions.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker for WOS Delivery Config sidecar (per ADR 0076 D-3)

targetWorkflow.description: Absolute URI of the WOS Kernel Document this delivery sidecar applies to. Processors load the sidecar when the kernel document's URI matches and resolve calendar lookups, notification template keys, and correspondence templates against this single document. When multiple delivery sidecars target the same workflow, the most recently effective one wins for calendar resolution (Governance S10.3); for templates and correspondence, processors merge by key and reject duplicates.

x-lm.intent: Binds this delivery sidecar to its parent kernel workflow

version.description: Version of this Delivery Config document. SemVer is RECOMMENDED. Increment when calendar holiday lists, notification template content, or correspondence templates change.

title.description: Human-readable name for this delivery configuration. Displayed in provenance audit trails and tooling when identifying the deployment-environment configuration for this workflow.

description.description: Human-readable description of this delivery configuration. Documents which jurisdiction's calendar applies, which notification templates are configured, and which correspondence channels are enabled for the target workflow.
examples: Federal benefits adjudication delivery configuration: US federal calendar with FY2026 holidays, APA-compliant adverse-decision notices, and inbound mail/portal correspondence templates.

calendar.description: Business calendar block. Defines business days, holiday schedules, and operating hours for SLA evaluation and temporal parameter resolution. Government workflows measure deadlines in business days, not wall-clock time: a 30-day response window excludes weekends and holidays. Consumed by Governance S10.3 (SLA evaluation) and Governance S13.3 (temporal parameter resolution). When absent, SLA evaluation uses wall-clock time.

x-lm.intent: Business calendar block: working days, holidays, operating hours (was wos-business-calendar sidecar)

notifications.description: Notification templates block. Defines reusable templates for notices generated during governance events: adverse decision notices, hold notifications, appeal acknowledgments, SLA warnings, and case status updates. Templates separate notice content from governance logic, enabling independent versioning, localization, and audit. Referenced by `notificationTemplateKey` (Governance S12.2) and `noticeTemplateKey` (Governance S3.1).

x-lm.intent: Notification templates block: notice bodies, channels, sections (was wos-notification-template sidecar)

correspondence.description: Correspondence metadata block. Declares the metadata schema for correspondence entries (letters, phone calls, emails, portal submissions, in-person interactions) stored in case state. The kernel's existing event mechanism handles correspondence events; this block defines structured metadata for consistent cataloging and retrieval without adding new event types or modifying lifecycle semantics.

x-lm.intent: Correspondence metadata block: entry templates and field path (was wos-correspondence-metadata sidecar)

timezone.description: IANA timezone identifier. All time calculations for this calendar use this timezone. Operating hours (when present) are interpreted in this timezone.

x-lm.intent: Determines the timezone for all business-day and operating-hours calculations

items.description: A day-of-week name (lowercase). A calendar date is a business day only when its day-of-week appears in workWeek and the date is not in the holidays list.

workWeek.description: Ordered list of working days. A date is a business day only if its day-of-week appears in this array and the date is not a holiday. Standard US federal work week: ['monday', 'tuesday', 'wednesday', 'thursday', 'friday'].

x-lm.intent: Declares which days of the week are business days for SLA calculation

holidays.description: Holiday schedule. Days listed here are non-working regardless of workWeek. Includes both fixed-date holidays (e.g., July 4) and floating holidays (e.g., third Monday of January).

x-lm.intent: Holidays that are non-working days regardless of whether they fall on a workWeek day

operatingHours.description: Working hours within a business day. When present and an SLA is specified in hours, the processor counts only elapsed time during these hours. When absent, a business day is the full 24-hour period.

x-lm.intent: Constrains SLA hour-counting to actual operating hours within each business day

effectiveDate.description: Date this calendar becomes effective (ISO 8601 date). When absent, the calendar is effective immediately.

expirationDate.description: Date this calendar expires (ISO 8601 date). A processor MUST ignore an expired calendar and fall back to wall-clock time.

appliesWhen.description: FEL expression evaluated against the case file to determine whether this calendar applies to a given case. Multi-jurisdiction workflows declare one calendar per jurisdiction and use this expression to route a case to the right one (Business Calendar §7.1). Evaluated whenever an SLA or temporal-parameter resolution requires a calendar; calendars whose expression evaluates false are excluded from composition. When absent, the calendar applies unconditionally to every case targeting the same workflow — equivalent to `appliesWhen: 'true'`. The expression sees the same evaluation context as governance FEL expressions: case-file fields, lifecycle state, actor metadata. See Kernel §7 for FEL grammar.

x-lm.intent: Routes a case to the right calendar in multi-jurisdiction workflows by FEL expression

name.description: Human-readable holiday name displayed in provenance audit trails and SLA calculation logs. Used to identify which holiday caused a business-day adjustment.

date.description: Fixed date for this holiday (ISO 8601 date). Required when 'rule' is not specified. One of 'date' or 'rule' MUST be present.

rule.description: Recurrence rule for floating holidays. Required when 'date' is not specified. Standard rules: 'nthWeekday(n, weekday, month)' for the nth weekday in a month, 'lastWeekday(weekday, month)' for the last weekday in a month. One of 'date' or 'rule' MUST be present.

observed.description: Whether this entry is an observed date rather than the actual holiday. When a fixed-date holiday falls on a non-working day, a separate entry with 'observed: true' marks the actual day off.

start.description: Start of the operating period (HH:MM in 24-hour format, interpreted in the calendar's timezone). The operating period is [start, end) — inclusive of start, exclusive of end. Hour-based SLA counters only accumulate time within this window.

x-lm.intent: Beginning of the daily operating window for hour-based SLA counting

end.description: End of the operating period (HH:MM in 24-hour format, interpreted in the calendar's timezone). MUST be strictly after 'start'. Hour-based SLA counters stop accumulating at this time each business day and resume at 'start' the next business day.

x-lm.intent: End of the daily operating window for hour-based SLA counting

templates.description: Named notification templates. Template keys are the identifiers referenced by notificationTemplateKey (Governance S12.2) and noticeTemplateKey (Governance S3.1) in governance documents.

sections.content: You have the right to appeal this decision.

caseHoldNotice.description: Notice that case processing is on hold pending applicant action.

sections.content: Your case is on hold because {{holdReason}}.

x-lm.intent: Named templates that governance mechanisms resolve via notificationTemplateKey and noticeTemplateKey

category.description: Template category. Determines which governance mechanism uses this template. 'adverse-decision': adverse decision notice (Governance S3.1-S3.2). 'hold-notification': hold notice (Governance S12.2). 'appeal-acknowledgment': appeal receipt confirmation (Governance S3.5). 'sla-warning': SLA deadline warning (Governance S10.3). 'case-status-update': general case status notification. 'resume-notification': notice that a case resumed from hold (Governance S12.3).

x-lm.intent: Classifies the template by governance event type to enforce category-specific requirements

description.description: Human-readable description of when this template is used and what regulatory obligation it satisfies. Referenced in audit logs.
examples: Notice of adverse benefits determination sent to applicant per APA adverse decision notice requirements.

subject.description: Subject line or title for the notification. MAY contain placeholder variables using {{variableName}} syntax.

sections.description: Ordered content sections composing the notification body. For 'adverse-decision' templates, sections MUST address: specific determination, individualized reason codes, appeal rights and filing deadline, and appeal instructions (Notification Template S4.4).

x-lm.intent: Ordered sections that compose the notification -- adverse-decision templates must include due process required sections

items.description: Variable name that must be present in the rendering context. Matches a {{variableName}} placeholder used in section content strings.

requiredVariables.description: Variables that MUST be present in the rendering context. If any required variable is missing, the processor MUST NOT send the notification and MUST record the failure in provenance.

items.description: A delivery channel identifier. 'postal': physical mail. 'email': electronic mail. 'portal': government web portal. 'sms': text message. 'in-app': in-application notification.

deliveryChannels.description: Channels through which this notification can be delivered. 'postal': physical mail. 'email': electronic mail. 'portal': web portal notification. 'sms': text message. 'in-app': in-application notification. When absent, delivery channel is implementation-defined.

localeRef.description: BCP 47 language tag reference to a locale-specific variant of this template. When the recipient's locale matches, the localized template is used instead of the default.

authority.description: Regulatory or statutory authority requiring this notification.

id.description: Section identifier unique within the template. Used in audit logs and accessibility anchors. Referenced by condition expressions to target specific sections.

title.description: Section heading displayed in the rendered notification. Rendered as a visible heading above the section content in postal, email, and portal output.

contentType.description: Type of content in this section. 'text': free-text with optional placeholders. 'structured': structured content with labeled fields. 'appeal-rights': standardized appeal rights block (must include filing deadline, review body, continuation status). 'action-required': describes actions the recipient must take. 'contact-information': contact details for questions.

x-lm.intent: Classifies the section content for rendering and validation of due process requirements

content.description: Content body. MAY contain placeholder variables using {{variableName}} syntax. Required when contentType is 'text' or 'structured'. For 'appeal-rights', 'action-required', and 'contact-information', content provides additional context beyond the structured elements.
examples: You have the right to appeal this decision within {{appealWindow}} of the date of this notice.
Please submit the following documents within {{expectedDuration}}: {{requiredAction}}

required.description: Whether this section MUST appear in the rendered notification. Default: true. For adverse-decision templates, due-process sections (determination, reasons, appealRights) MUST be required.

condition.description: FEL expression that controls section visibility. When the expression evaluates to false, the section is omitted from the rendered notification. Evaluated against the standard FEL evaluation context (Kernel S7.3).

correspondenceField.description: Dot-separated case state field path where correspondence entries are stored as an array. The workflow processor appends correspondence entry objects to this path when correspondence events are recorded in provenance. MUST refer to an array-typed field in the case state.

x-lm.intent: Identifies which case state field holds the array of correspondence entries

entryTemplates.description: Templates defining the metadata structure for correspondence entries. Each template declares the channel, direction, actor type, and required fields for a category of correspondence.

examples.description: Physical mail received from applicant or representative

x-lm.intent: Templates that define what metadata each category of correspondence entry must carry

id.description: Unique template identifier. Referenced by correspondence entries via templateRef.

description.description: Human-readable description of the correspondence category this template covers, used in tooling and audit summaries.
examples: Physical mail received from applicant or representative
Phone call with applicant or representative

channel.description: Communication channel. 'in-person': face-to-face contact. 'phone': telephone call. 'mail': physical mail. 'email': electronic mail. 'portal': web portal submission or message. 'fax': facsimile transmission. Extensible via x- prefixed values in the extensions property.

x-lm.intent: Declares the communication channel for this correspondence category

direction.description: Direction of the correspondence. 'inbound': received by the agency. 'outbound': sent by the agency.

actorType.description: Who sent or received the correspondence. 'applicant': the primary case subject. 'representative': authorized representative acting on behalf of the applicant. 'third-party': external party (e.g., employer, medical provider). 'system': automated system-generated correspondence. 'agency': agency staff.

x-lm.intent: Identifies the actor role for this correspondence category

items.description: Name of a correspondence entry field that MUST be present for entries using this template, supplementing the base required fields (templateRef, channel, direction, actorType, contentRef, summary, timestamp).

requiredFields.description: Fields that MUST be present in a correspondence entry using this template, beyond the base metadata (templateRef, channel, direction, actorType, contentRef, summary, timestamp).

templateRef.description: The 'id' of the EntryTemplate this correspondence entry conforms to. Processors validate that the referenced template exists in the sidecar's 'entryTemplates' array and that all template 'requiredFields' are present in this entry.

channel.description: Communication channel used for this specific correspondence entry. MUST match the 'channel' declared in the referenced EntryTemplate. Allowed values: 'in-person', 'phone', 'mail', 'email', 'portal', 'fax'.

direction.description: Indicates whether this correspondence was received by the agency ('inbound') or sent by the agency ('outbound'). MUST match the 'direction' in the referenced EntryTemplate.

actorType.description: Role of the actor who sent or received this correspondence. MUST match the 'actorType' in the referenced EntryTemplate. Allowed values: 'applicant', 'representative', 'third-party', 'system', 'agency'.

contentRef.description: Claim check reference -- a URI or storage reference for the actual content. The content itself is not stored in case state; this is a pointer to it.

x-lm.intent: Pointer to the actual correspondence content stored outside case state

summary.description: Human-readable one-line summary describing the substance of this correspondence event, used in audit logs and case timelines. Should be concise and actor-neutral.
examples: Adverse decision notice mailed to applicant

relatedTaskRef.description: Optional reference to a task this correspondence relates to.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 date-time recording when the correspondence event occurred, used for ordering case history and SLA calculations. MUST be in UTC or include a timezone offset.

ExtensionsMap.description: Vendor extension data attached to this node. All keys MUST start with 'x-' (see Kernel §10.6). The reserved namespace 'x-wos-*' is for WOS Working Group use only; third-party extensions MUST use a unique vendor prefix (e.g., 'x-acme-', 'x-vendor-'). Processors MUST ignore unknown extension keys to preserve forward compatibility. Extension values are unconstrained — any JSON value is valid, but authors are encouraged to document the shape in their vendor spec.

JsonSchemaUri.description: Optional JSON Schema URI enabling editor validation and autocompletion. When present, editors (VS Code, IntelliJ, etc.) validate the document against this schema. Omit in production documents; the authoritative schema URI is derived from the document type marker (e.g., '$wosDelivery': '1.0'). Used for author-time tooling only — runtime processors MUST ignore this field.
